replies.author: @RetardationAwareness
replies.text:
That's not fair though... that would mean every judge needs to sentence every person who breaks the law to life in prison. 
They should be disbarred if they let people who've committed 3 sex crimes do less than the max time. 
They could simplify this by creating a 3 strike rule. Judges wouldnt have a say in that case, it'd be a minimum sentence.

replies.author: @RetardationAwareness
replies.text:
That's really the only logical solution, make the laws harsher on crime, then judges would have to be as well.
That way, there'd be no liability for the judges to worry about, resulting in unnecessarily harsh sentences for everyone, nor would there be unnecessarily lenient sentences for violent offenders. 

I think a lot of crimes deserve more than one chance, but there should be harsh punishments for people who steal, and especially people who physically harm others. 
I don't think being an addict who got caught with your stash should result in a harsher punishment than attacking other people, for instance. 
But attacking people 3 times is more than losing your temper while out at a bar or something. 
That's someone who needs to be taught a lesson

replies.author: @OrchidsOutOfChaos
replies.text:
@terryfetters8555  What happens then, the same as it always happens, periods of lucidity in the society followed by a period resembling a coma after which the pendulum sways back.
What needs to happen is for Trump's policies being encoded into law but that requires a cooperative Senate and House.
If Trump can have of the support of both houses, his policies can be made law.
But if Republicans don't take both chambers, the pendulum will most assuredly just continue it's arc.
The mid-terms are vitally important in determining where we go from here.

replies.author: @OrchidsOutOfChaos
replies.text:
@terryfetters8555 I like your take on things. I too feel my views are constitutionally centered.
I came at it from a different path though. For me, from my first reading wasn't due to being bright but more so, curious to know how it all fits together.
I began to see the text forming a type of lens under which the concepts embodied by the text in the document can be examined.
That said, as to your situation, John Nash was crazy.  The most interesting people I know are crazy but have found ways to integrate with others and the world around them better.
Just remember, only the truly crazy are not allowed to have keys.
